name: Pumpkin Empanadas
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg beaten with 1 tablespoon water for glaze
2 tablespoons Sugar mixed with 1 teaspoon cinnamon
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
2. Cut in the shortening and butter using a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Drizzle the brandy and milk over the flour mixture and stir until distributed.
4. Form into a flat disc, wrap in plastic wrap, and chill for 30 minutes.
5. Preheat oven to 400°F.
6. To make the filling, blend together the pumpkin, brown sugar, cinnamon (or cinnamon), anise leaves, nutmeg, egg, and vanilla.
7. Roll out the chilled dough thinly on a floured board or pastry cloth.
8. Cut into 4- or 5-inch circles.
9. Place about 2 tablespoons of filling on the lower half of each dough circle.
10. Fold over the top half and press the edges together with a fork.
11. Brush tops with egg wash and place on a baking sheet.
12. Bake for 15 to 18 minutes, or until golden.
13. While still warm, sprinkle with the cinnamon sugar mixture.
14. These can be baked up to 1 day in advance. Before serving, warm in a 350°F oven for 8 minutes. Makes 14 to 16 empanadas.
